danger_signals.visual_form: Large primate sprite hanging from center vines, active animation
danger_signals.meaning: Primary threat; rampaging causes insect swarms to emerge

danger_signals.visual_form: Buzzbees, Beespies, inchworms, attacker bees, Kabutomushi beetles, butterflies in motion
danger_signals.meaning: Active environmental hazards; some attempt to steal flowers (reduce bonus points)

danger_signals.visual_form: Queen Beespies shatter into deadly pieces when hit
danger_signals.meaning: Fragmented hazard state; increased threat level after destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Flowers at bottom of screen being carried away by insects
danger_signals.meaning: Resource depletion warning; direct loss of end-of-round bonus accumulation

safety_signals.visual_form: Can object falling from vines when Donkey Kong is sprayed past certain point
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability/advantage state; accelerates Donkey Kong upward faster, easing level completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Player character positioned on stable ground levels beneath vine structure
safety_signals.meaning: Operational base; safe firing position for bug spray offensive

safety_signals.visual_form: Donkey Kong forced to top of screen or all insects eliminated
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone achieved; transition to next repeating level sequence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bug spray can weapon; exterminator profession
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bottom platform levels; greenhouse garden setting
Center-screen vine structure; primary vertical axis

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Multiple flying creature types (bees, beetles, butterflies, inchworms)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Queen Beespies spawn fragmented attack pieces; distinct behavioral types per insect species
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Airspace between platforms and vines; flower collection points at screen bottom

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Busy screen with multiple animated sprites; insects in flight paths; Donkey Kong animated on vines
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Insects attempt flower theft; Donkey Kong hangs and resists spray pressure; Stanley must maintain offensive fire

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: DK sprite position rises on vine structure; Super Sprayer can appears when threshold passed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced progression toward level completion; accelerated upward movement when power-up triggered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen clears of flying hazards or DK reaches top boundary
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level complete transition; score bonus calculated from flowers retained; progression to next repeating level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score display milestone; mode transition indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game shifts to 'survival of the fittest' difficulty escalation mode after first extra life earned

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Natural/plant-based background environment; flowers as collectible/defensive resource
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Insect swarm motivated by flower presence; Stanley's exterminator role contextually justified; Donkey Kong rampaging causes ecological disruption

environmental_storytelling.example: Greenhouse garden location establishes Stanley's profession context; Donkey Kong's intrusion creates insect pest outbreak rather than kidnapping/platforming conflict
Flowers displayed at screen bottom as visual stake; player can directly observe insect theft, creating tangible loss mechanic and narrative urgency
Vine structure in center with Stanley below and Donkey Kong above creates environmental narrative of invasion from above; platforms represent defensive positions
Bug spray can identifies Stanley as pest control professional; spray mechanic communicates extermination gameplay rather than violent confrontation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active threat; resists spray pressure; must be continually sprayed upward to clear level
Patrol airspace; some attempt to intercept and steal flowers from bottom of screen
Shatter into multiple deadly fragments that become independent hazards
Fly autonomous attack patterns; target flower resources
Decorative/minor threat; part of insect swarm composition
Temporary pickup; increases spray pressure magnitude and speed; limited duration effect
Player-controlled; maintains spray on Donkey Kong while managing insect threats; defends flower resource

notes: Donkey Kong 3 represents a tonal departure from series predecessors: the conflict is ecological/pest-control rather than rescue-based. World-state communication relies on resource visibility (flower count), enemy positioning hierarchy (DK above, Stanley below), and insect behavior patterns (theft attempts). The 'survival of the fittest' mode after 50k points suggests escalating difficulty through behavioral intensification rather than visual restructuring. Greenhouse setting functions as primary environmental storytelling device; absence of Mario and shift from platforming to spray-based combat creates distinct faction dynamic from earlier Donkey Kong games.
sources_used: wikipedia: Donkey Kong 3 (gameplay section, plot section)
